lineage.description: Lineage entries describing data flow declared for this job definition. Each entry identifies a target entity and the sources that feed into it.

LineageEntry.description: Describes data flowing into a target entity from source entities, at entity and/or column granularity.

namespace.description: The namespace of the target entity.

name.description: The name of the target entity.

type.description: The type of the target entity. DATASET for dataset entities. JOB for job entities, used when the job itself is the data consumer (sink) or producer (generator) — i.e., when there are no output datasets (sink) or no input datasets (generator).

inputs.description: Entity-level source inputs. An empty array explicitly means the target has no upstream source (e.g., a data generator).

fields.description: Column-level lineage. Maps target field names to their source inputs. Only meaningful when the target type is DATASET.

LineageFieldEntry.description: Column-level lineage for a single target field.

inputs.description: Source entities and/or fields that feed into this target field.

LineageInput.description: A source entity that feeds data into a lineage target.

namespace.description: The namespace of the source entity.

name.description: The name of the source entity.

type.description: The type of the source entity. DATASET for dataset entities. JOB for job entities, used when a job is the origin of data (e.g., a generator job that creates data without reading from any input dataset).

field.description: The specific field/column of the source dataset. When present at entity-level inputs, represents a dataset-wide operation (e.g., GROUP BY column). When present at field-level inputs, represents the source column that feeds into the target column.

transformations.description: Transformations applied to the source data.

LineageTransformation.description: A transformation applied to source data in a lineage relationship.

type.description: The type of the transformation. Allowed values are: DIRECT, INDIRECT.

subtype.description: The subtype of the transformation, e.g., IDENTITY, AGGREGATION, FILTER, JOIN, GROUP_BY, WINDOW, SORT, CONDITIONAL.

description.description: A string representation of the transformation applied.

masking.description: Whether the transformation masks the data (e.g., hashing PII).
